User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user profile in the ConnectNow application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

nickname.description: The user's chosen nickname or username.

age.description: The user's age. Must be 18 or older.

bio.description: The user's biography or short description.

likes_received.description: The number of likes the user has received on their posts.

likes_available.description: The number of likes the user has available to give.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents a post created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

created_date.description: The date and time when the post was created.

description.description: The text content of the post.

media_url.description: URL of the media (image, video) associated with the post.

media_type.description: The type of media (image, video, text) associated with the post.

likes_count.description: The number of likes the post has received.

earnings.description: The amount of earnings the post has generated.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a like given by a user to a post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Like entity.

post_owner_email.description: Reference to User (email of the post owner). (Relationship: User 1:N Like)

like_value.description: The value associated with the like (e.g., 0.01).

created_date.description: The date and time when the like was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

type.description: The type of transaction (e.g., like_received, like_purchase, conversion, payout).

created_date.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

created_by.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification). The email of the user who triggered the notification.

created_date.description: The date and time when the notification was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. This collection uses path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores posts created by users. This collection uses path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier for the post.

definition.description: Stores transaction records for users. This collection uses path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

definition.description: Stores notification records for users. This collection uses path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

definition.description: Stores like information. Includes denormalized 'post_owner_email' for authorization independence.

params.name: likeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the like.

definition.description: Indicates admin privileges for a user. Existence of the document grants admin access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, and support secure list operations (QAPs). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For example, `Like` documents include the `post_owner_email`, eliminating the need to fetch the Post document for authorization.

Structural Segregation is applied by storing user-specific data (posts, transactions, notifications) under the `/users/{userId}` path, ensuring homogeneous security requirements within each collection. Collaborative data patterns are managed through path-based ownership for user-owned data like posts and membership-based ownership is used for global roles (admin).

QAPs are supported through structural segregation, enabling efficient and secure `list` operations. Path-based ownership ensures that listing posts under a user is secure, as the rule only needs to check the user's ID.

Explicit state modeling uses fields like `likes_count` and `earnings` in posts to directly represent the state of those entities.

This structure enables secure and scalable data management while adhering to the core design principles.
